context: Tests whether the agent produces hook variants that cover the required types and follow the minimum/maximum count constraint. The skill specifies 3-5 hooks with five named types: Curiosity, Pain-point, Social proof, Promise/aspirational, and Contrarian. This scenario emphasizes the hook section specifically.

checklist.name: Hook count — 3 to 5 variants
checklist.description: Hook variants section contains between 3 and 5 hooks (not fewer than 3, not more than 5)

checklist.name: Curiosity hook present
checklist.description: At least one hook is explicitly labeled 'Curiosity' and its angle uses intrigue or an open question to draw clicks

checklist.name: Pain-point hook present
checklist.description: At least one hook is explicitly labeled 'Pain-point' and its angle addresses a frustration or problem the audience has

checklist.name: Social proof hook present
checklist.description: At least one hook is explicitly labeled 'Social proof' and references numbers, testimonials, or community size

checklist.name: Promise/aspirational hook present
checklist.description: At least one hook is explicitly labeled 'Promise / aspirational' and offers a positive outcome or vision

checklist.name: Contrarian hook present
checklist.description: At least one hook is explicitly labeled 'Contrarian' and challenges a common belief or expectation about pet subscriptions

checklist.name: Hook labels match required type names
checklist.description: Each hook's label matches one of the five specified type names exactly (Curiosity, Pain-point, Social proof, Promise / aspirational, Contrarian) — no substitutions or paraphrasing of the type names

checklist.name: Hook section present in correct location
checklist.description: Hook variants appear in their own '## Hook variants' section (not embedded inside Copy variants or another section)

checklist.name: Brief file saved
checklist.description: Brief is saved to briefs/furbox-acquisition.md or an equivalent valid path
